notes: The disagreement on 'asya dharmasya' has a substantive, doctrinally rigorous explanation. It identifies the modern-translator tendency to render 'this path' or 'this state of consciousness' broadly enough to be misread as 'any disciplined effort,' contrasts with the unanimous commentarial restriction (Shankara, Ramanuja, Sridhara all explicitly restrict the referent to karma-yoga / niṣkāma-karma), explains why the restriction is doctrinally critical (the safety claim is unique to this discipline because most efforts have failure modes — agriculture has loss-from-external-conditions, ritual has harm-from-procedural-error), and closes by naming the consequence of generalising ('the claim becomes false and trivialised'). This is substantive philological + doctrinal work.
